title: When Evaluating Information: - Seek Opposing Views: Actively Search for Evidence That Challenges Your
subtitle: Challenge Confirmation Bias
description:
When evaluating information:
- Seek opposing views: Actively search for evidence that challenges your beliefs.
- Ask questions: “What would change my mind about this?”
- Stay curious: Approach topics with an open mind, looking for new perspectives.

Example: If you believe a certain diet works, read studies that critique it.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the claim
howTo.summary: Identify a specific claim you want to evaluate. Use precise wording to ensure clarity, as vague claims invite vague searches.

howTo.title: Ask the disconfirming question
howTo.summary: Formulate the question, 'What would change my mind about this?' Write a brief statement in the Brali app to clarify your current belief.

howTo.title: Conduct a focused opposing search
howTo.summary: Spend 10-20 minutes searching for credible evidence that contradicts the claim. Look for peer-reviewed studies, expert critiques, and high-quality articles.

howTo.title: Synthesize findings
howTo.summary: Summarize your findings in one sentence, noting any changes in confidence levels. Decide on an action based on the strength of the opposing evidence.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app, including the claim, sources, confidence changes, and any actions taken. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/challenge-confirmation-bias-checklist/.
